{"step_by_step_thinking": "The query asks whether case-mix based reimbursement stimulates the development of process-oriented care delivery. The provided documents from MEDCORP and UMLS do not explicitly define or discuss 'case-mix based reimbursement' or its impact on 'process-oriented care delivery'. The MEDCORP documents discuss various reimbursement models such as fee-for-service, capitated payment, episode-of-care, and value-based care (e.g., ACOs), and mention incentives for quality, coordination, and cost efficiency. These models may indirectly support process-oriented care by promoting care coordination and quality metrics, but there is no direct evidence or statement linking case-mix reimbursement specifically to process-oriented care delivery. The UMLS documents are focused on obstetric conditions, procedures, and classifications and do not address reimbursement models or care delivery processes. Therefore, based on the available information, there is insufficient evidence to confirm or deny the effect of case-mix based reimbursement on process-oriented care delivery.
